com.tonic.jaloc.memory

Class MappedAllocator

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.MappedAllocator
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    AllocationOwner, NativeAllocator, java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class MappedAllocator
extends java.lang.Object
implements NativeAllocator, AllocationOwner, java.lang.AutoCloseable
    An allocator backing each block with a memory-mapped temp file the kernel deletes at process exit, so pages are OS-reclaimable under memory pressure instead of pinned like malloc. Blocks use disk transiently while live. Accessing a mapped page whose backing file hits an I/O error (disk full, device loss) can terminate the JVM.

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description

      void close()
      Refuses further allocations.
      static MappedAllocator ephemeral()
      Creates a spill allocator over temp files in the system temp directory.

      Creates a spill allocator over temp files in directory.
      boolean isOpen() 
      long mappedBytes() 
      void release(com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.AllocationRecord allocation)
      Releases the allocation's memory.

    • Method Detail

      • ephemeral

        public static MappedAllocator ephemeral()
        Creates a spill allocator over temp files in the system temp directory.
        Returns:
        the allocator
        Throws:
        java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException - if file mapping is unavailable on this JVM

      • ephemeral

        public static MappedAllocator ephemeral(java.nio.file.Path directory)
        Creates a spill allocator over temp files in directory.
        Parameters:
        directory - the directory to place backing files in
        Returns:
        the allocator
        Throws:
        java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException - if file mapping is unavailable on this JVM

      • clearRequired

        public boolean clearRequired()
        Specified by:
        clearRequired in interface NativeAllocator
        Returns:
        true if fresh blocks need explicit zeroing; allocators handing out pre-zeroed memory return false

      • allocate

        public com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.MemoryBlock allocate(long bytes,
                                                            int alignment)
        Description copied from interface: NativeAllocator
        Allocates bytes with the given alignment.
        Specified by:
        allocate in interface NativeAllocator
        Parameters:
        bytes - the payload size
        alignment - a positive power of two
        Returns:
        the block

      • release

        public void release(com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.AllocationRecord allocation)
        Description copied from interface: AllocationOwner
        Releases the allocation's memory.
        Specified by:
        release in interface AllocationOwner
        Parameters:
        allocation - the record to free

      • isOpen

        public boolean isOpen()
        Specified by:
        isOpen in interface AllocationOwner
        Returns:
        true while this owner can still back its allocations

      • mappedBytes

        public long mappedBytes()
        Returns:
        the total bytes currently mapped by live blocks

      • close

        public void close()
        Refuses further allocations. Outstanding blocks stay mapped until they close or the cleaner reclaims them; the kernel deletes every backing file at process exit regardless. Safe to call more than once.
        Specified by:
        close in interface java.lang.AutoCloseable
